title: TaxProfile
description: Generic, shareable tax profile for the workbench-ynab plugin. This schema is intentionally GENERIC: nothing in it is specific to one taxpayer. A real taxpayer's numbers become one INSTANCE of this schema (see M3-5), never part of the schema or any prompt. The live instance is read by the SKILLS (never by the vendored YNAB MCP) from ~/.claude/plugins/data/workbench-ynab-claude-workbench/tax-profile.json. All money amounts in this profile are in DOLLARS, not YNAB milliunits (divide YNAB milliunits by 1000 to get dollars).
$comment: Data-dir convention: the owner's live tax profile lives OUTSIDE this repo at ~/.claude/plugins/data/workbench-ynab-claude-workbench/tax-profile.json, following the same pattern workbench-core uses for ~/.claude/plugins/data/workbench-core-claude-workbench/config.json (read via jq from hooks). See assets/tax/README.md. This file is config-as-data only — no executable logic.

$readme.description: Optional human-facing onboarding note carried by the bundled example so a user who copies it sees, in the file itself, where the live instance belongs, that it is never committed, and how to populate it. Declared here so an instance carrying it still satisfies additionalProperties:false; it is a $-prefixed annotation, so the profile loader (M3-3) strips it pre-merge — it never reaches the resolved profile or any tax calculation. Free-form lines of text.

schemaVersion.description: Version of this tax-profile schema the instance targets, so future migrations can detect and upgrade older instances. Accepts a string (e.g. "1" or "1.0.0") or an integer.

filingStatus.description: Federal filing status. single = Single; mfj = Married Filing Jointly; mfs = Married Filing Separately; hoh = Head of Household; qw = Qualifying Surviving Spouse (formerly Qualifying Widow(er)).

taxYear.description: The tax year this profile applies to. The profile is year-aware; lookups such as standardDeductionByYear are keyed by this year.

standardDeductionByYear.description: Standard deduction lookup keyed by filing-status string, then by four-digit tax-year string. Values are dollar amounts — not YNAB milliunits (divide YNAB milliunits by 1000 to get dollars).

additionalProperties.description: Year-keyed map of standard-deduction amounts for one filing status.
Standard deduction in dollars, not YNAB milliunits (divide YNAB milliunits by 1000 to get dollars).

businessEntities.description: Sole-prop / pass-through business entities the taxpayer files for. Each entity maps its YNAB expense category-groups onto schedule lines. Use placeholder display names only (e.g. "Business A") — never a real business name.

id.description: Stable, opaque identifier for this entity, referenced by mappings and reports. Placeholder example: "biz-a".

displayName.description: Free-text label for the entity. MUST be a placeholder in the schema/defaults/examples (e.g. "Business A") — never a real business name.

schedule.description: IRS schedule this entity files on, e.g. "C" for sole proprietorship.

scheduleLineMap.description: Mapping from this entity's YNAB expense category-groups onto schedule line items. The DETAILED shape of this object is owned by issue M3-2 (mapping engine); this schema only requires it to be present as an object. Kept open (additionalProperties: true) so M3-2 can define its internal structure without bumping this schema.

itemized.description: Schedule A (itemized deductions) configuration. Money amounts here are dollars, not YNAB milliunits (divide YNAB milliunits by 1000 to get dollars).

medical.description: Schedule A medical & dental expenses. Only the portion above thresholds.medicalAgiPercent of AGI is deductible (that percentage lives under thresholds, not here).

categoryGroups.description: YNAB category-group names whose transactions count as medical expenses.

salt.description: Schedule A state-and-local taxes (SALT): income/sales tax plus property tax, capped at saltCap.

saltCap.description: Maximum deductible SALT in dollars, not YNAB milliunits (divide YNAB milliunits by 1000 to get dollars). Federal cap is 10000 (5000 if filing MFS).

categoryGroups.description: YNAB category-group names whose transactions count as state/local taxes.

interest.description: Schedule A deductible interest: home mortgage interest and HELOC interest.

categoryGroups.description: YNAB category-group names whose transactions count as deductible mortgage/HELOC interest.

charitable.description: Schedule A charitable contributions (cash and non-cash gifts to qualified organizations).

categoryGroups.description: YNAB category-group names whose transactions count as charitable contributions.

adjustments.description: Schedule 1 adjustments to income (above-the-line deductions). Money amounts here are dollars, not YNAB milliunits (divide YNAB milliunits by 1000 to get dollars).

seTaxHalfDeduction.description: Deduction for one-half of self-employment tax. This amount is DERIVED from computed SE tax (thresholds.seTaxRate), so it carries no dollar figure here — only an enabled toggle.

enabled.description: Whether to apply the one-half-of-SE-tax adjustment.

amount.description: Student loan interest paid, in dollars, not YNAB milliunits (divide YNAB milliunits by 1000 to get dollars). Subject to the IRS annual cap and phase-out.
Deductible traditional-IRA contribution, in dollars, not YNAB milliunits (divide YNAB milliunits by 1000 to get dollars).

thresholds.description: Tunable tax thresholds and rates used by the engine. Rates are fractions (0.075 = 7.5%); saltCap is a dollar amount.

medicalAgiPercent.description: Medical-expense AGI floor as a fraction (0.075 = 7.5%). Only medical expenses above this fraction of AGI are deductible on Schedule A. Not a dollar amount.

seTaxRate.description: Combined self-employment tax rate as a fraction (0.153 = 15.3% = 12.4% Social Security + 2.9% Medicare). Not a dollar amount.

saltCap.description: Default SALT deduction cap in dollars, not YNAB milliunits (divide YNAB milliunits by 1000 to get dollars). Federal cap is 10000 (5000 if filing MFS). itemized.salt.saltCap, when present, overrides this.

quarterlyEstimatedDueDates.description: Federal quarterly estimated-tax due dates for the tax year, as explicit {quarter, month, day} parts. FORMAT CHOICE: parts (not ISO strings) are used so the engine can resolve the calendar year per quarter — Q1-Q3 fall in the tax year (Apr 15 / Jun 15 / Sep 15) while Q4 falls in January of the FOLLOWING year (Jan 15). The consumer combines each part-set with the correct calendar year; weekend/holiday shifting is the engine's responsibility, not this data's.

month.description: Calendar month of the due date, 1 (January) through 12 (December).

day.description: Calendar day of month of the due date, 1 through 31.

overrides.description: User-override layer. The profile loader (issue M3-3) deep-merges these values ON TOP OF the bundled default US ruleset, so a user can change individual thresholds, deductions, or mappings without restating the whole ruleset. Shape mirrors a partial of this profile; kept open (additionalProperties: true) because any subset of the ruleset may be overridden. Money amounts here are dollars, not YNAB milliunits (divide YNAB milliunits by 1000 to get dollars).
